title: Articles: A, An, The & Zero Article
definition: Articles are words placed before nouns to show whether we mean something specific (definite) or general (indefinite). English has three articles: a, an, and the — plus cases where no article is used (zero article).
formula: a/an + Singular Countable Noun (general)  |  the + Specific Noun (definite)
rules: Use 'a' before words starting with a consonant SOUND: a book, a university (starts with 'yoo' sound).
Use 'an' before words starting with a vowel SOUND: an apple, an hour (silent 'h').
Use 'the' when both speaker and listener know exactly which thing is meant: Close the door (a specific door already known).
Use 'the' for unique things: the sun, the moon, the internet.
Use 'the' for superlatives and ordinal numbers: the best, the first.
Use zero article (no article) with plural general nouns, uncountable nouns, and most proper nouns: Dogs are loyal. I love music. I live in India.
examples: I saw a movie last night.
She is an honest person. (silent 'h', vowel sound)
The book on the table is mine.
The Earth revolves around the sun.
He is the tallest boy in class.
Dogs are loyal animals. (zero article - general plural)
I live in India. (zero article - proper noun)

commonMistakes.why: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound (silent h), so it needs 'an'.

commonMistakes.wrong: I want to become an teacher.
commonMistakes.right: I want to become a teacher.
commonMistakes.why: 'Teacher' starts with a consonant sound, so it needs 'a'.
General/abstract statements about life don't take 'the'.

commonMistakes.wrong: I play a cricket every day.
commonMistakes.why: Names of games/sports usually take zero article.

quiz.question: Choose the correct article: 'She bought ___ umbrella.'
Choose the correct article: 'He is ___ MBA graduate.'
quiz.explanation: 'MBA' is pronounced 'em-bee-ay', starting with a vowel sound.

quiz.question: ___ sun rises in the east. (definite, unique object)
quiz.explanation: 'The' is used for unique things like the sun.

quiz.sentence: I saw an unicorn in the story.
quiz.correction: I saw a unicorn in the story.
quiz.explanation: 'Unicorn' starts with a consonant sound ('yoo'), so it needs 'a'.

quiz.question: Which sentence correctly uses zero article?
quiz.explanation: Abstract nouns used generally take zero article.
